key_concerns: Random allocation reported; experimenter blind to condition for some assessments; SCR is objective; outcome assessor blinding to condition during exposure not fully specified.
Random assignment confirmed; brief period of incomplete blinding plausible due to instructions; otherwise low risk.
Pre-registration not located; outcome reporting scattered across measures with selective emphasis on recovery-window contrasts.
Pre-registered; null result faithfully reported; complete outcome ascertainment; adolescent sample with strong age-matched controls.
Open-label single-arm pilot; no control group; commercial COI (NeuroGen Technologies); ~13 completers; selective focus on within-subject pre-post effect sizes.
N=30 likely underpowered (Button et al. 2013); ROI definition not pre-registered; cluster thresholds pre-Eklund (2016); single laboratory.
N=11; small-sample inferential limits; foundational study; reported correlations may be inflated.
N=11; small but appropriate inference; concurrent SCR cross-validates fMRI.
DCM model selection involves substantial researcher degrees of freedom; partial reanalysis of prior data.
Same lab as foundational paper; ROI strategy partially data-driven; modest N.
Independent lab; clear language manipulation; appropriate ROI strategy; replication of opposite-direction effect well-documented.
Independent lab; clean design; replicated in Study 2; data and code on OSF.
Pre-registered; independent lab; conceptual replication of Nook et al.
Independent lab; four studies with consistent direction; total N=405.
Independent lab; intensity moderator clearly tested.
Large k=385; predictor coding documented; results moderate-confidence due to between-study heterogeneity inherent to ALE.
Large k=386; clear inclusion criteria; outputs are activation density rather than effect size.
Reappraisal-focused; affect labeling not directly synthesized.
Connectivity meta-analysis; left-vlPFC convergence relevant for affect labeling lateralization claim.
